title: gladiolus longicollis subsp. longicollis
intro: Gladiolus longicollis subsp. longicollis, also known as the Red Corn Lily, is a perennial plant species in the Iridaceae family. It is known for its bright red flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Gladiolus longicollis subsp. longicollis include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, such as skin conditions and infections.
